findings: Path traversal (cmdStatusAll): NONE. Story dir names come from fs.readdirSync itself — filesystem entry names cannot contain path separators and '.'/'..' are never returned, so path.join('.keel','state', ent.name, 'manifest.json') cannot escape the root. Entries are only read; names are echoed via JSON.stringify (safe encoding, no injection sink). '--all', '.lock', and '*.tmp' entries are explicitly filtered.

Corrupt-manifest error field: INFO. error: e.message from JSON.parse can echo up to ~16 raw bytes of the corrupt file's content — verified empirically on Node v26 ("Unexpected token 'o', \"{\"a\": tok_SECRET_\"... is not valid JSON"). Local file read by a local CLI printed to the same user's terminal — same trust domain, no direct risk; only matters if status --all output ever feeds remote sinks (Slack/dashboards).

Injection/exec: diff adds no exec/spawn/eval and no new interpolation into executed commands; test additions call the pre-existing engine() harness with array args and write fixtures only to tmp dirs. XSS n/a (no web surface); auth/IDOR n/a (local single-user CLI).
Sensitive data: status --all entries project exactly {story_id, scope, current_phase, halted} — pipeline metadata only, no credentials/PII/tokens; no secrets touched by the diff.

Scanner inventory (honest): composer-audit, phpstan, snyk, sonar-scanner all skipped per prescan.json (not applicable / not configured on this Node repo). npm-audit prescan-skipped for 'no lockfile' was remediated this phase per KEEL-101 precedent: ephemeral lockfile via 'npm i --package-lock-only --ignore-scripts' (metadata only), then 'npm audit --package-lock-only' ran clean — 0 vulnerabilities (exit 0) — and the ephemeral lockfile was deleted, tree restored.
Findings: 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 1 LOW (pre-existing, carried from KEEL-101: no committed lockfile makes SCA point-in-time, not introduced by this diff), 1 INFO (corrupt-manifest error snippet, above).

Blast radius: .keel/graph/codegraph.json absent — reverse-dependency query skipped; manual check: cmdStatusAll has a single caller (the dispatcher intercept) and its consumer is the commands/health.md fleet sweep. Memory: lessons.md L-1 reviewed — no user-facing path strings added by this diff. CJIS presence flag: NO.
Verdict: PASS (0 HIGH) — report written to docs/security/KEEL-102-security-report.md.
decisions: Consumed the orchestrator prescan instead of re-running skipped-not-applicable scanners; only npm-audit was re-run because its skip reason (no lockfile) was fixable tooling, remediated with the same ephemeral-lockfile method recorded in the KEEL-101 run.

Rated the corrupt-manifest error-message content INFO not LOW/MEDIUM: empirically confirmed the snippet is capped (~16 chars), the data source is a local repo file, and the output sink is the local terminal in the same trust domain; recommendation recorded to genericise the message if fleet output ever gains remote sinks.
Carried the no-committed-lockfile SCA caveat forward as pre-existing LOW rather than re-attributing it to this change.
